rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CLAUDIA B. RICHARDSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/03/2021. 1) No original bank statement verifying the ending balance is on file. The statement filed on 12/24/2020 is dated 11/30/2020. 2) Neither the petition caption nor the notice of hearing included a request to renew the petitioner's authority to pay fees on account. Also, no fee schedule appears to have been filed in compliance with Probate Code sections 2643.1(a) & 2614.7.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD REASOR EMMONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/20/2021. 1) The date of the filing of the request for special notice stated in paragraph 1 of the petition appears incorrect. 2) The date of the order for probate appointing the co-administrators stated in paragraph 3 appears incorrect. 3) The proofs of service attached to both allowances filed on 10/29/2015 and referenced in paragraph 5 do not show the date and place of mailing. 4) The nature of each claim listed in paragraph 5 must be described. CRC 7.403(3). 5) Paragraph 7c alleges notice was given to the Franchise Tax Board before (but not after) letters were issued to the petitioners as general personal representatives. Probate Code section 9202(c). 6) The date of the filing of the corrected final inventory and appraisal stated in paragraph 13 appears incorrect. 7) The amount of the supplemental inventory and appraisal stated in paragraph 13 appears incorrect. 8) The final account period ends on 12/31/2019, although this petition was not filed until 08/31/2020. Were there disbursements or other transactions during the 8-month period? What is the amount of the current cash on hand? 9) Detailed time sheets showing 400 hours of the co-administrator's services claimed to be extraordinary must be filed. 10) Detailed time sheets showing Roberta Dangaran's services claimed to be extraordinary must be filed, together with a statement required by CRC 7.703(e)(1)(A) & (C). 11) Exhibit F to the attorney's declaration Part 3 filed on 09/03/2020 must specify the document title and filing date for each FileandServeXpress cost.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BROOKSEY M ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. issue is request to appoint petitioner as personal representative when she has already been appointed probate referee in this case. counsel was asked to file a supplement if he wanted to move forward with this petition, but nothing has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CHARLES EMANUEL PERI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. the documents show the parents lived in San Francisco in 1900 but in Marin County in 1910. the petition alleges Charles Emanuel Peri was born in 1903 in San Francisco. while the death certificate supports the date of birth, there is no document that supports San Francisco as the place of birth. Petitioner may be asked what efforts he made in Marin County to locate a birth certificate and what efforts he made to establish when the family moved. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.
